name: Eekso — The Broken Foyer
dmNotes: ENTRY SCENE. The thesis of the whole dungeon lands here: a place owned, renovated and abandoned by many hands, and one god left standing on purpose. Torm intact among three ruined Old Gods is the necromancer's signature — do not explain it, just let them look at it. The rugs are pit traps down to the Pitfall Prison; falling is the intended alternate entrance, not a punishment. Investigation DC 15 to spot, DC 13 Dex to catch the lip. Fall = 30 ft: 3d6 bludgeoning + 2d10 piercing on the spikes (DC 13 Dex halves the spike damage only). If someone drops, switch to 'Eekso — The Pitfall Prison' and split the party — that is a feature.
scriptText:
# The Broken Foyer

The air past the threshold is dry and still, and colder than the tunnel behind you. Dust hangs in it — undisturbed, the way dust only hangs in rooms nobody walks through.

Somebody built this hall to impress. Somebody else knocked half of it down. Somebody after *that* patched the walls with cheaper stone and a worse hand, and then they left too.

The fortress has been owned, and re-owned, and forgotten, more times than the stone can remember.

> [!dm] Texture to deal out as they poke around — every detail says *many owners, none recent*:
> - Three different mortars in one wall; a doorway bricked up, then broken open again
> - A banner rotted to threads, the sigil unreadable
> - Boot-scuffs in the dust going **one direction only** — deeper in
> - No bodies. No blood. Not here.

## The Four Gods

{{music:curious}} Four pedestals stand along the far wall, and on three of them there is nothing left worth naming.

The first is a pair of feet and a shattered ankle. The second has been struck so hard the head lies twenty feet away, face-down. The third is powder and a hand.

The fourth is *perfect*.

He stands with his sword point-down and both palms open on the pommel, head bowed — and someone has kept him clean. There is no dust on his shoulders. Somebody has been coming here, for a long time, to wipe this one statue down.

> [!dm] **Torm. God of self-sacrifice.** Religion DC 10 to name him; anyone with a faith background knows him cold.
>
> Do NOT explain the significance. Let them stand there and work out that the vandal and the caretaker are the same person.
>
> - If pushed: the three ruined gods were struck with something *heavy and blunt* — not weapons, not time
> - The pedestal is scrubbed. There are old brown flecks in the seam where the base meets the floor that did not scrub out

## The Rugs

{{music:unease}} Rugs and carpets lie across the floor of the hall — good ones once, layered thick, running toward the doors like a path laid out for a guest.

> [!dm] **They are pit traps.** Under each is an open shaft to the Pitfall Prison, spiked at the bottom.
>
> - **Spot it:** Investigation DC 15 — the rugs don't lie flat, they *sag*. Anyone actively prodding the floor finds it automatically.
> - **Step on it:** DC 13 Dex save to catch the lip and go prone at the edge instead.
> - **The drop:** 30 ft. **3d6 bludgeoning** + **2d10 piercing** from the spikes. DC 13 Dex halves the spike damage only. {{music:drop}}
>
> A dropped PC lands in the **Pitfall Prison** — switch scenes and run them split. This is a legitimate way in, not a mistake. The necromancer built a front door for people who don't look down.

## The Old Camp

Tucked against the wall, out of the traffic path: a camp that was made carefully and left in a hurry.

- Bedrolls still laid out. A cold ring of stones, ash gone to grey powder
- Packs cut open and rifled — **from the outside**, after they were left
- Scratches in the stone beside the bedrolls, a tally, stopped mid-count

> [!dm] Whoever camped here got to sleep, and did not get to pack. Don't say it — let them notice the packs were opened by someone else.

## East — The Dining Hall

Through the doors to the east, the hall opens into what was obviously a dining room, and just as obviously hasn't fed anyone in a lifetime.

- Long tables shoved aside or broken down for firewood that was never burned
- Stools stacked in a corner by someone tidy, decades ago
- Dry cups. Dry bowls. A rot-black smear in one, and nothing else

> [!dm] **This room is empty and it is meant to be empty.** It's the exhale before the garden. Give them thirty seconds of finding nothing, and let them relax.
>
> If they need something: a child's carved toy under a table, far from anything else. No explanation, ever.

ideas.text: Someone wipes Torm's statue down regularly — no dust on the shoulders, and the party can see fresh finger-tracks in the grime if they look close
Every footprint in the dust goes deeper in. Nothing has ever walked back out.
The tally scratched by the bedrolls stops mid-count — the last mark is a partial stroke
The rugs are laid like a runner leading to the doors — the trap is dressed as hospitality

entities.name: Statue of Torm (intact, tended)
entities.note: God of self-sacrifice. Three siblings destroyed; this one is dusted daily. The necromancer's signature.

entities.name: The rug pit traps
entities.note: Investigation DC 15 / Dex DC 13. 30 ft: 3d6 bludgeoning + 2d10 piercing.

entities.name: The abandoned camp
entities.note: Left in a hurry; packs rifled from outside afterward.
